segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They crossed the ditch at a dry spot Vex found upstream and cut through a copse of alder that smelled of cat-pee and river iron. The day leaned toward afternoon; clouds stacked to the west in slate wedges.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They found an abandoned charcoal clamp on the lee side of a low hill -- a stone circle still black with old burns, sheltered from the wind by a crumbled wall that had been somebody's idea of useful forty years ago. Good sightlines south and west.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Cover north. The kind of place people stop at when they know they need to stop and won't admit it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin's arm had cooled to a sulk. Durgan watched his own shadow not move. Vex used a shard of Caelin's glass -- she'd pocketed it from the ditch, because Vex pocketed everything -- to trim a hanging thread from her sleeve with a surgeon's care.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric set down his bag and looked at Vex.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Rib."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It's fine."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You winced when you broke that spear. Sit down."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I said -- "

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I know what you said."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He was already opening the bag.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Sit down."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She sat. He ran two fingers along the rib line with clinical detachment, found the break by her sharp intake of breath, and pronounced it the same as before -- cracked, not displaced, nothing to do but strap it and stop doing stupid things with it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She didn't comment on the last part. He didn't expect her to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: While he worked, Caelin sat against the clamp's stone wall and let the lattice quiet.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The glass trick had cost less than the scream-cast -- far less -- but the cost was still measured in pain and precision and the knowledge that every expenditure drew from a balance he couldn't check. He flexed his fingers.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The coin trick: heat left, cold right, hold the border. He managed five seconds before the spiral collapsed. Progress. Incremental, painful, insufficient progress.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric checked Caelin's arm wrap next -- didn't touch the scale, just the bandaging. His fingers were impersonal and thorough, the way a mechanic checks an engine: looking for wear, for heat, for the signs that something internal was failing before the external showed it.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You're holding tension in the shoulder,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Favoring the arm means the trapezius compensates. Another week of this and the shoulder freezes. I've seen it in soldiers who take a lance wound and won't let anyone set the arm properly."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Holding,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, in the tone of a man noting a weather gauge.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That's not an answer."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The words landed heavier than Aldric probably intended -- or maybe exactly as heavy as he intended, because the look he gave Caelin was the look of a man who'd heard soldiers say holding right up until the moment they fell down and didn't get up.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Veln's Post,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The thing that came through the vault. What was it?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric packed his bag with the precision of routine.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I don't know what it was. I know what it did. It moved through sealed stone as if the seals weren't there. It didn't kill the men -- it unmade them. No blood. No wounds. They just... stopped. Like someone had found the thread that held them together and pulled it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He fastened the clasp.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I was in the east tower. I heard the silence when the screaming stopped. That's the part I remember most clearly -- the silence. Thirty-seven men, and then quiet, and then the sound of something large settling back down, like a cat that's caught what it wanted and gone back to sleep."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "And you've been walking toward it ever since."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Toward the answer. Which might be the same thing."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stood, bag over his shoulder, and looked north.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Two years of walking. Lot of roads. Lot of patients. Lot of villages where the people were scared of something they couldn't name and the dogs wouldn't go outside after dark."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He met Caelin's eyes.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Whatever's in that plateau is waking up incrementally. The thralls at Millbrook are the edge. The thing at Veln's Post was closer to the center. And whatever's at the actual center -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He paused.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Well. That's what we're going to find out."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You don't have to come,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You could stay. There are villages that need a surgeon."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "There are always villages that need a surgeon. There will always be villages that need a surgeon."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric's voice was even, unhurried, the voice of a man who'd thought about this every day for two years and had stopped being surprised by his own answer.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "But there's only one Depthspire. And only one chance to be in the room when the door opens. I wasn't in the room at Veln's Post. I've spent two years carrying that absence. I'd like to stop."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex, who'd been lying on her good side pretending to rest, opened one eye.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "He'll do,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said to Caelin.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric raised an eyebrow.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "That's high praise from her,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Accept it and move on before she changes her mind."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They rested another twenty minutes, then packed and moved northeast through the thinning countryside, the ironwood treeline a dark smear on the approaching horizon.
